title: Ochna caracasana
taxonomy: Ochna caracasana is a species of flowering plant in the family Ochnaceae. It is native to Venezuela, and its classification places it within the order Malpighiales. This order is part of the clade Rosids, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. The family Ochnaceae is a small group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Ochna caracasana is an example of a plant that has adapted to these environments, with characteristics such as glossy leaves and showy flowers.
